content.problemStatement:
Consider NNN parallel wires running from left to right. The wires are numbered 111 through NNN from top to bottom. There are MMM devices called balancers, numbered 111 through MMM from left to right. For each valid iii, balancer iii connects wires xix_ixi​ and yiy_iyi​ (xi<yix_i \lt y_ixi​<yi​). Let's call such a structure of wires and balancers a balancing network.

Each balancer can be directed either upwards or downwards. The directions of all balancers define the state of the balancing network.

Consider a token that starts moving to the right along some wire, from a point on that wire which is to the left of all balancers. During this process, the token encounters each balancer exactly once. Whenever the token encounters a balancer iii, the following happens:


if the token is moving along the wire xix_ixi​ and balancer iii is directed downwards, the token moves down to the wire yiy_iyi​
otherwise, if the token is moving along the wire yiy_iyi​ and balancer iii is directed upwards, the token moves up to the wire xix_ixi​
in all other cases, the token does not move to a different wire
afterwards, the token keeps moving to the right
We say that a state of the balancing network:


transforms a wire iii into a wire jjj, if a token that starts moving along the wire iii ends up on the wire jjj after passing through all MMM balancers
unifies wires iii and jjj, if it transforms both of these wires into the same wire (possibly different from each of them)
is kkk-non-unifying, if there is a set of kkk distinct wires such that this state does not unify any pair of wires from this set
For a given balancing network, please find any ⌈N2⌉\left\lceil \frac{N}{2} \right\rceil⌈2N​⌉-non-unifying state or determine that there is no such state.
constraints: 1≤xi<yi≤N1 \le x_i \lt y_i \le N1≤xi​<yi​≤N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104

`Explanation**Example case 1:** The state where all balancers are directed upwards ("^^^") transforms wire 111 into itself, wire 222 into wire 111 and wire 333 into wire 222. We can see that this state does not unify wires 111 and 333 (or wires 222 and 333), thus it is 222-non-unifying. Another 222-non-unifying state of this balancing network is "^^v". The remaining 666 states are not 222-non-unifying.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi​ and yiy_iyi​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no ⌈N2⌉\left\lceil \frac{N}{2} \right\rceil⌈2N​⌉-non-unifying state for the given balancing network, print a single line containing the string `"failure"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print a single line containing a string with length MMM. This string should describe some ⌈N2⌉\left\lceil \frac{N}{2} \right\rceil⌈2N​⌉-non-unifying state. For each valid iii, its iii-th character should be either '^' if the iii-th balancer is directed upwards or 'v' if the iii-th balancer is directed downwards.
